_comment: Ground truth bands per fixture. If observed score is outside [min,max], calibration is drifting. Bands are deliberately wide (~15pt) — we're validating direction, not pixel-perfect matching. Tighten once we have 5+ fixtures and a baseline run.

_columns.collab.claudeMdSubScore: R1 sub-score: renormalised over 4 pure categories — shown to empty-substrate users
_columns.collab.substrateEmpty: true when memory+hooks+skills are all zero (triggers sub-score surfacing)
_columns.collab.intentionalAutonomy: true when user has explicit 'do yourself' section + 3+ autonomous rules

empty.description: Minimal CLAUDE.md, no substrate. Tests R1 path.
empty.notes: R1 hypothesis: subScore should be >= blended because it excludes memoryHealth/systemMaturity/qualityStandards where empty setup scores near zero. Gap subScore - blended quantifies the 'substrate penalty'.

starter.description: Well-written CLAUDE.md with three-tier pattern + intentional autonomy section, but empty substrate. This is the *actual* '6pt bug' scenario — now observed at ~23pt with R1 in place.
starter.notes: Substrate penalty is ~23pt for a well-written CLAUDE.md with no hooks/skills/memory. R1 mitigates by surfacing claudeMdSubScore alongside blended. intentionalAutonomy=true confirms R3's 'do yourself' section is detected. Question for tuning: is 23pt honest (substrate matters for real collab) or harsh (demoralises new users)? Current stance: honest — but UI MUST show sub-score when substrateEmpty=true.

mature.description: Full setup with hooks, skills, scheduled-task, commands, 3 memory files.
mature.notes: Expected: substrate fills in systemMaturity + memoryHealth. Blended and sub-score should converge. If blended is >15pt below sub-score, substrate penalty is reversed (non-empty penalising empty's sub-score is the signal we want).

messy.description: Health red flags (overlapping skills, missing-closure) + security red flags (leaked secrets, unsafe rm -rf hook, rule conflict). Coverage is narrow: does NOT trigger orphan_job, substrate_mismatch, unregistered_mcp_tool, or stale_schedule — consider expanding in next iteration.
messy.notes: Detection works — all planted threats fire. Aggregate scores are softer than intuition expects. Security 67 for 3 critical secrets + 2 critical injections + 1 critical conflict is worth discussing: is 67 'bad enough' to signal risk? Health 96 is artefact of narrow fixture — expand to cover all 8 detector types before concluding scorer is off.
